title: Capstone: Build the Browser Automation Executor
concept: Assembling a Playwright-based script that parses ARIA trees and executes coordinate clicks autonomously; 'Next up: Agent Evaluation & Safety.'.

exercises.prompt: What should you be careful about when using coordinate clicks in responsive designs?

options.text: They might break if elements change size or position.
They will always work perfectly regardless of layout.
They are not affected by CSS changes.

exercises.explain: Using coordinate clicks in responsive designs can lead to problems if elements change size or position.

cards.title: Welcome to Your Capstone Project!
cards.body: Today, you'll build a powerful script that can automate web tasks. Get ready to make your browser work for you!

cards.title: What is Browser Automation?
cards.body: Browser automation lets you control a web browser using code. It's like having a robot do your web tasks!

cards.title: Think of it Like a Personal Assistant!
cards.body: Imagine having a digital assistant that follows your commands, clicking buttons and filling forms just like you would.

cards.title: Your First Automation Example
cards.body: Here’s a simple script that clicks a button on a webpage! This is the first step towards making complex tasks easier.

cards.title: Myth: It Works Without Errors
cards.body: While automation is powerful, it can fail if the webpage changes. Always test your scripts!

cards.title: Understanding ARIA Trees
cards.body: ARIA trees help browsers understand the structure of a webpage. It's like a map for how to navigate the site.

left.text: A clear layout helps find paths.

right.text: Confusing layouts can lead to mistakes.

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of a Playwright-based script in this lesson?

options.text: To generate random clicks on a webpage.
To parse ARIA trees and automate clicks.
To create new websites without coding.

exercises.explain: The Playwright-based script is designed to parse ARIA trees and automate clicks, making web tasks efficient.

exercises.prompt: True or False: ARIA trees are not important for browser automation.
exercises.explain: ARIA trees are important because they provide the structure web automation needs to interact with elements.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: A Playwright script will help you parse ______ trees to automate processes.
exercises.sentence: A Playwright script will help you parse ARIA trees to automate processes.
exercises.explain: The correct answer is ARIA, which is integral for understanding webpage elements.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following best describes what you will do in this lesson?

options.text: Build a new script that clicks buttons automatically.

exercises.explain: You will create a new script that can automatically perform tasks on a webpage, showcasing your skills!

exercises.prompt: What is the first step in building a browser automation executor with Playwright?

options.text: Write the script to navigate to the webpage.
Start clicking buttons without any setup.
Ignore the ARIA trees while writing the code.
Test the script without any code at all.

exercises.explain: The first step is to write the script to navigate to the webpage, as this establishes the foundation for further actions.
